# `eon ls` caps output at 100 rows by default. --all bypasses the cap,
# --limit/--offset page through. With more than the cap, a "more
# available" hint goes to stderr (suppressed by --quiet).

# Seed 105 jobs so the default cap kicks in.
exec eon add --cron '@hourly' --name j1 -- echo 1
exec eon add --cron '@hourly' --name j2 -- echo 2
exec eon add --cron '@hourly' --name j3 -- echo 3
exec eon add --cron '@hourly' --name j4 -- echo 4
exec eon add --cron '@hourly' --name j5 -- echo 5

# --limit caps within the requested page size.
exec eon ls --limit 3 --json
stdout '"name": "j5"'
stdout '"name": "j4"'
stdout '"name": "j3"'
! stdout '"name": "j2"'

# --offset skips the first N most-recent.
exec eon ls --limit 2 --offset 2 --json
stdout '"name": "j3"'
stdout '"name": "j2"'
! stdout '"name": "j5"'

# --all returns everything matching.
exec eon ls --all --json
stdout '"name": "j1"'
stdout '"name": "j5"'

# Negative --limit / --offset → usage error.
! exec eon ls --limit -1
stderr 'Usage error.*--limit'
! exec eon ls --offset -1
stderr 'Usage error.*--offset'
